D12 PsychologyL2 UnderstandReference

Synesthesia Prevalence — Type Breakdown

~4% of the population has at least one type; grapheme-colour (1.4%) and weekday-colour (0.9%) are the most common; ordinal-linguistic personification much rarer

Simner et al. (2006) screened 1,190 adults: 4.4% had at least one synesthesia type. Twin studies suggest ~50% heritability; the genes implicate axon-pruning regulation between sensory cortices (Asher 2009). Most synesthetes report it as a positive feature, not a deficit.

Pick a type to inspect its prevalence + description.
